claims.name: export.integrity.v1
claims.description: Verifies signed export-manifest integrity: content hash, signature, sibling artifacts.

claims.name: export.scope_identity.v1
claims.description: Verifies the export's manifest identity fields against the signed payload shape: export_id, project_id, export_type, format, compressed, and record_count.

claims.name: governance_chain.local_continuity.v1
claims.description: Recomputes each chain entry's record_hash and verifies prev_hash continuity within the bundled chain window.

claims.name: permit_chain.delegation_denied_correctly.v1
claims.description: Adjudicates that a permit.delegated_denied event correctly denied delegation under pinned authority-envelope.v0 comparator semantics.

claims.name: closure.signature.v1
claims.description: Verifies closure-record Ed25519 signature against the permit_binding_signing key purpose.

claims.name: closure.digest_consistency.v1
claims.description: Verifies provider_response_digest_v1 / client_response_digest_v1 against bundled lifecycle events.

claims.name: closure.dispatch_binding.v1
claims.description: For closure_v2, verifies dispatch_request_digest_v1 against the permit's binding_request_hash.

claims.name: workflow.declaration_signature.v1
claims.description: Verifies workflow-declaration Ed25519 signatures emitted alongside Vanta evidence exports.

claims.name: workflow.amendment_signature.v1
claims.description: Verifies workflow-amendment signatures and amendment-version ordering.

claims.name: workflow.effective_intent_hash.v1
claims.description: Re-derives effective_intent_hash = SHA-256(declaration.intent_json ‖ ordered amendments at decision time) and matches against permit.workflow_state_json.

claims.name: workflow.permit_snapshot.v1
claims.description: Verifies workflow_state_json snapshots carried on permits.

claims.name: incident.bundle_manifest.v1
claims.description: Verifies incident bundle manifest_version 2 file-set (workflow_declarations.jsonl, workflow_amendments.jsonl) and fails closed on unknown manifest versions.

claims.name: checkpoint.composite_hash.v1
claims.description: Recomputes the integrity checkpoint chain_heads composite hash.

claims.name: checkpoint.signature.v1
claims.description: Verifies the integrity checkpoint Ed25519 signature.

claims.name: checkpoint.tsa_imprint.v1
claims.description: Verifies the embedded RFC 3161 timestamp MessageImprint when present. TSA chain authenticity is a separate opt-in (--tsa-ca-bundle).

claims.name: workflow_evidence.sibling_integrity.v1
claims.description: Verifies the sibling workflow_evidence file hash matches the signed export manifest's sibling_artifacts.workflow_evidence entry.

claims.name: checkpoint.scope_state.v1
claims.description: Verifies a signed scope-state sidecar against an anchored checkpoint, trust root, supported predicate grammar, and pinned Merkle commitment profile.

claims.name: export.scope_faithfulness.v1
claims.description: Adjudicates whether a signed export pack is a scope-faithful slice of the recorded chain for the declared population or declared sample up to the declared checkpoint boundary, and supports auditor testing of the completeness assertion for the declared evidence population and the accuracy assertion for records inside the declared scope.

claims.name: permit.decision.v1
claims.description: Verifies the issuance-time permit decision canonical payload, canonical hash, and Ed25519 signature under the permit-binding trust root; mutable row state is not adjudicated as the issuance decision.

claims.name: permit.revoked.v1
claims.description: Verifies a strict post-cutover permit.revoked event signature, permit/project identity binding, opaque actor identity, and v1 immediate-effect semantics where effective_at equals revoked_at.

claims.name: permit.dispatch_absence_after_revocation.v1
claims.description: Performs scope-faithful absence adjudication for post-revocation dispatch.egress_bound events within a declared export scope anchored to supported revocation, scope-state, and export scope-faithfulness evidence; it is falsifiability-oriented scoped adjudication, not a global completeness claim over all history.

claims.name: permit.operator_approval.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 operator_approval slot as a distinct issuer-organization dual-control signature over its slot-specific canonical payload.

claims.name: permit.counter_signature.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 counter_signature slot as a distinct buyer-principal pre-dispatch signature over execution intent and permit binding inputs, including canonical execution-intent hash reconciliation.

claims.name: permit.audit_attestation.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 audit_attestation slot as a distinct buyer-principal post-hoc signature bound to a known audit batch.

claims.name: permit.operator_approved.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 operator_approval slot as a distinct issuer-organization dual-control signature over its slot-specific canonical payload.

claims.name: permit.counter_signed.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 counter_signature slot as a distinct buyer-principal pre-dispatch signature over execution intent and permit binding inputs.

claims.name: permit.audit_attested.v1
claims.description: Verifies a Permit v2 audit_attestation slot as a distinct buyer-principal post-hoc signature bound to a known audit batch.

artifact_formats.name: permit_v2
artifact_formats.note: Additive Permit v2 signature-envelope slots are verified as distinct claims.

artifact_formats.name: checkpoint_v1
artifact_formats.note: Keel-internal artifact; verified against bundled checkpoint signing keys.

artifact_formats.name: incident_bundle_v2
artifact_formats.note: Keel-internal artifact; manifest_version 2 with workflow files.

artifact_formats.name: workflow_evidence_v1
artifact_formats.note: Keel-internal artifact; sibling of audit-export bundles.

artifact_formats.name: checkpoint_scope_state_v1
artifact_formats.note: Signed scope-state sidecar for checkpoint-bound scope reconciliation.

failure_codes.note: This subset is hand-curated for capability_schema_version 1.0; a future schema version may extract programmatically from tests/code.

proves: Post-signing tampering of any element in the permit lifecycle (input, dispatch, provider response, client response, closure record) is detected.
Signature validity against bundled or supplied public keys (Ed25519).
Record-hash chain continuity within bundled chain entries; sequence-inversion detection.
Closure-binding consistency for dispatched executions (closure_v2 dispatch_request_digest_v1 cross-reference).
Workflow declaration and amendment signature validity (workflow_intent_v1).
Pack-pinned semantic integrity: declared semantic artifacts resolve and validate against a permanent, append-only allowlist.
does_not_prove: Pre-signing manipulation by a privileged Keel operator at evidence-creation time.
Historical TSA certificate revocation status — TSA chain validation is point-in-time and opt-in (--tsa-ca-bundle).
Backend authorization correctness for the underlying executions (the verifier checks evidence, not the authorization decision logic itself).
Provider or tool actually performing the action correctly in the real world — the verifier validates recorded evidence.
requires_for_strong_assurance: Hardware-backed signing (TEE/HSM) for pre-signing protection — a separate capability tier above this verifier.
Live trust-root anchoring via --public-key-url against the production endpoint.
Trust-root rotation via the refresh-keys subcommand.

out_of_scope.reason: Permit attests that a decision was made by the runtime. Identity authentication occurs upstream of the verifier.

items: Side effects beyond the recorded request/response
out_of_scope.reason: The verifier validates recorded evidence; it cannot validate world-state outside the recorded artifacts.

items: Chain entry hash format v2+ (not yet defined)
out_of_scope.reason: Future wire-format versions; this verifier covers the v1 family. New formats require new spec versions and verifier support.
Permit records a decision; verifier validates the record. Policy logic is implementation-defined per Permit spec.

verdict_durability.guarantee: A version-pinned evidence pack receives reproducible adjudication: any future verifier release must resolve the exact pinned semantics by (id, sha256) and reproduce the same claim verdicts for that pack, or explicitly decline. It must never silently reinterpret a prior pinned claim.
verdict_durability.not_guaranteed: Verdicts are not asserted to be 'correct for all time'. A pinned semantic later found flawed is corrected by issuing a new versioned semantic, never by editing a released artifact.
